_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create our own stories and texts by using or adapting ideas, characters, settings and language features from stories we have read.

_source.w: We are learning to create our own stories and texts by using or adapting ideas, characters, settings and language features from stories we have read.
y: All writers learn from other writers — adapting ideas is how we develop our own style.
Using techniques from published texts gives us a strong foundation for our creative work.
This process helps us experiment and take creative risks in our writing.
eg: We write a story using a plot structure we noticed in a book we enjoyed.
We create a character inspired by one we read about, but in a new setting.
We borrow a language technique — like using short sentences for tension — from an author we admire.
We adapt a fairy tale by changing the setting to a modern Australian city.
We write a poem using the same structure as one we studied in class.
We create a new adventure for a character from a book, imagining what happens next.
